summary: Iteration 47 — Backend testing of refactored per-ATIVO Planos de Inspeção + Inspecao auto-load. 20/20 tests pass for happy-path CRUD + auto-load + backward compat. However a CRITICAL bug was discovered in the inspection auto-load query that selects the WRONG plan when an ativo has multiple plans of different tipos.

pass_fail_matrix.POST /api/inspecoes tipo=inspecao auto-l: PASS (when ativo has only one plan)

critical.issue: When an ativo has multiple plans of different tipos, the auto-load picks the WRONG plan. Verified: ativo with plans {tipo=lubrificacao, versao=1} and {tipo=inspecao, versao=99}, POST /inspecoes tipo=lubrificacao returns the INSPECAO plan's checklist (7 items) instead of the LUBRIFICACAO plan (2 items).

action_items:
CRITICAL: Fix /app/backend/server.py lines 1180-1182. The Mongo query dict has TWO `$or` keys in the same literal — Python dict semantics make the second `$or` (status filter) silently overwrite the first (categoria/tipo filter). Result: the tipo/categoria match is dropped from the query and the most-recent active plan of ANY tipo wins via the sort. Fix by combining via `$and`:

  plano = await db.planos_inspecao.find_one(
      {"organization_id": org_id, "ativo_id": data.ativo_id, "deleted_at": None,
       "$and": [
           {"$or": [{"categoria": cat_busca}, {"tipo": cat_busca}]},
           {"$or": [{"status": "ativo"}, {"status": {"$exists": False}}]}
       ]},
      {"_id": 0},
      sort=[("versao", -1), ("created_at", -1)]
  )

After fix, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration47_rca_dollar_or.py — should pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1180-1182 — duplicate `$or` keys in the same dict literal silently drop the tipo/categoria filter. This breaks the core 'per-ATIVO plan resolution by tipo' contract of the refactor. The /api/planos-inspecao/resolver endpoint (line 998-1032) does NOT have this bug — it uses a single $or correctly. The bug is only in the inline auto-load logic inside POST /inspecoes.

server.py:1198-1216 — checklist normalization between PlanoPergunta.{texto,tipo_campo,obrigatoria,valor_min,valor_max} and ChecklistItem.{descricao,tipo,obrigatorio,tolerancia_min,tolerancia_max} works but has divergent field names; consider unifying these in a future cleanup to avoid the per-field if/else mapping.
server.py:1175 (tipo_to_categoria) — missing key 'inspecao' is mapped via dict.get(tipo_str, tipo_str), so 'inspecao' falls through; OK but explicit mapping would be safer.

models.py:444-462 PlanoPerguntaCreate — has BOTH 'obrigatoria' (new) and 'obrigatorio' (compat), as well as 'texto'/'descricao' and 'tipo_campo'/'tipo'. Backward compat is fine, but document precedence in code comments; right now precedence is enforced in server.py at line 940-943 (texto wins over descricao) and 1203-1205 (texto OR descricao; obrigatoria OR obrigatorio).
server.py:912 GET /api/planos-inspecao — accepts `categoria` query parameter but tests indicate the new contract is tipo-based. Consider documenting that `categoria` is a legacy alias.
server.py:927 — sort by 'categoria' on list; with the new tipo-first model, consider also/instead sorting by 'tipo'.

success_rate.backend: 95% (20/21 incl. RCA test failing as expected)

seed_data_creation: All TEST_-prefixed plans were created and then soft-deleted by the cleanup class. 3 test inspecoes were created against the demo ativo (not cleaned — minor data residue, but harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 new GET endpoints work, CRUD on /planos-inspecao works, auto-load on /inspecoes works for the SINGLE-plan-per-ativo case. The CRITICAL gap is the multi-plan-per-ativo case at server.py:1180-1182 (duplicate $or). The RCA reproduction test is at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration47_rca_dollar_or.py and currently FAILS — after main agent applies the fix, re-run that file plus the main suite at test_iteration47_planos_per_ativo.py.
rca of the issue: Mongo query at server.py:1180-1182 uses a Python dict literal with two `$or` keys. Python evaluates left-to-right so the second `$or` (status filter) overwrites the first (categoria/tipo filter). Effective query becomes `{ativo_id, deleted_at, $or:[status==ativo, status missing]}` — i.e. ANY active plan for the ativo, sorted by versao desc + created_at desc. Reproduction: create plan A {tipo:lubrificacao, versao:1} then plan B {tipo:inspecao, versao:99} for same ativo; POST /inspecoes tipo=lubrificacao incorrectly returns plan B's 7-question checklist. Mitigation: merge the two `$or` clauses into a single `$and` array as shown in action_items.
